hypothesis: Fine-tuning pre-trained ML interatomic potentials (such as eSEN or eqV2) on stress-tensor-augmented active learning datasets generated specifically along SSNEB-converged pathways will disproportionately reduce stress prediction errors relative to energy and force errors, and this targeted stress correction will enable fully ML-only SSNEB calculations to achieve energy barriers within 5% of DFT reference values without any DFT restart step, thereby eliminating the hybrid workflow bottleneck identified in this paper.
